title: sphaeropteris capitata
intro: Sphaeropteris capitata, also known as the Tree Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Cyatheaceae, native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, derived from its sap and leaves. The health benefits of Sphaeropteris capitata include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the leaves or sap to create infusions, tinctures, or topical ointments for various health applications.
